title: All Souls' Day
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## November 3, 2022 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

November 3, 2022 was Thursday of All Saints' Week, following All Saints' Day on November 1st. All Souls' Day (November 2nd) had just passed the previous day, making this sermon directly relevant to the liturgical commemoration of the dead. The lectionary would have featured readings focused on remembrance of the faithful departed, likely from the Wisdom of Solomon or similar commemorative texts. The church calendar was in Ordinary Time, specifically in the season between Pentecost and Advent.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating significant internal divisions over LGBTQ+ inclusion and theological interpretation; General Convention had recently (2022) affirmed marriage equality and expanded ordination opportunities for transgender and non-binary clergy. The Diocese of Colorado was led by Bishop Glasspool (who had taken office in 2019), an openly lesbian bishop whose presence and leadership represented the church's progressive positioning on these issues. Saint John's Cathedral, as the diocesan seat, would have been at the center of these ongoing denominational conversations.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical early November weather patterns, with snow and cold temperatures arriving. The November 2022 midterm elections were just days away (November 8th), dominating local political discourse with competitive races for governor (Republican Jena Griswold vs. Democrat Jared Polis), Senate, and congressional seats. Capitol Hill, where the Cathedral is located, was an increasingly gentrified neighborhood experiencing rapid development and demographic shifts that would have been visible to the congregation.

NATIONAL NEWS

The midterm elections were the dominant story, with Republicans expected to gain significant House seats while Democrats were performing better than historically typical for an out-of-power party. The FBI had recently executed a search warrant at Mar-a-Lago related to classified documents (August 2022), with ongoing legal developments dominating cable news. Gas prices had peaked in summer but remained elevated compared to historical norms, affecting household budgets across the nation.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine, begun in February 2022, was in its ninth month with brutal fighting continuing in eastern Ukraine and recent Russian retreat from Kherson. The UK was experiencing severe political turmoil with a third prime minister in six months (Liz Truss had just been replaced by Rishi Sunak on October 25th). Global energy crisis fallout from the Ukraine war was affecting Europe heading into winter, with inflation and energy prices dominating international headlines.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a state of pre-election anxiety and exhaustion, with cultural polarization at a high point and misinformation concerns heightened. The midterms were framed as an existential choice for democracy's future, creating significant emotional and social tension within congregations and communities. The mood was somber and forward-looking with uncertainty, making a sermon on remembrance of the dead and the communion of saints particularly resonant as a grounding spiritual practice.
